questions.question: What is the equivalent internal resistance of a series combination of n cells?

options.label: A
options.text: the arithmetic mean of their internal resistance

options.label: B
options.text: the product of their individual internal resistance

options.label: C
options.text: the sum of their individual internal resistance

options.label: D
options.text: the sum of the reciprocal of their individual internal resistance

answer.label: C
answer.text: the sum of their individual internal resistance

questions.explanation: The equivalent internal resistance of a series combination of n cells is just the sum of their internal resistances.

questions.question: What do we call a cell that induces a spontaneous redox reaction to create a flow of electrical charges?
questions.explanation: A Galvanic cell or Voltaic cell induces a spontaneous redox reaction to create a flow of electrical charges, or electricity.

questions.question: Which of the following cells is the basis of Non-rechargeable batteries?
questions.explanation: Non-rechargeable batteries are examples of Galvanic cells.

questions.question: What do we call the cell that requires an outside electrical source to drive the non-spontaneous redox reaction?
questions.explanation: An Electrolytic cell is one kind of battery that requires an outside electrical source to drive the non-spontaneous redox reaction.

questions.question: What kind of cell is the mobile phone battery when it is being charged?
questions.explanation: While the battery is being used in the device it is a galvanic cell function (using the redox energy to produce electricity). While the battery is charging it is an electrolytic cell function (using outside electricity to reverse the completed redox reaction).
